title: Micranthemum callitrichoides
taxonomy: Micranthemum callitrichoides is a species of flowering plant in the family Plantaginaceae. It is native to the southeastern United States and is commonly known as Brazilian pygmyweed or pygmyweed. Micranthemum callitrichoides is a relatively small, aquatic plant that thrives in environments with slow-moving water. This plant is often used in aquariums and other aquatic settings due to its ability to thrive in a variety of conditions. Within the family Plantaginaceae, Micranthemum callitrichoides is classified in the genus Micranthemum.

intro: Micranthemum callitrichoides, also known as Callitricha or Pink Starflower, is a low-growing, spreading, and perennial plant native to South America, belonging to the family Plantaginaceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, skin irritations, and infections. Bioactive constituents of Micranthemum callitrichoides include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used in herbal preparations such as teas and infusions, as well as in topical applications like creams and salves, to promote wound healing and soothe skin conditions.
